title: British Roast Trifle
description: A gooey, cozy British dessert featuring all-purpose flour, roast, and Ayurvedic-Rajasic-friendly ingredients, perfect for a Yorkshire-inspired comfort food meal.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 1808C (3508F).
Line a 23cm (9-inch) deep dish with parchment paper.
In a medium bowl, whisk together the flour and sugar.
Add the softened butter and rub it into the flour mixture until it resembles breadcrumbs.
Press half the breadcrumb mixture into the prepared dish.
Bake for 20 minutes, or until lightly golden brown.
Remove from the oven and let cool slightly.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the eggs and vanilla extract.
Pour the egg mixture over the cooked base and smooth the top.
Bake for a further 15-20 minutes, or until the mixture is set and lightly golden.
Allow the trifle to cool completely before assembling the layers.
Cut the cooled trifle into thick slices and serve with a dollop of whipped cream and a sprinkle of chopped pistachios.
To make the custard, combine the milk and double cream in a saucepan and heat until just simmering.
In a separate bowl, whisk together the egg yolks and a little of the warm milk mixture to temper them.
Gradually pour the tempered egg yolk mixture into the warm milk mixture, whisking constantly.
Return the saucepan to the heat and cook, stirring constantly, until the custard thickens.
Remove from the heat and stir in the chopped pistachios.
To assemble the trifle, start with a layer of the cooled bread and butter base, followed by a layer of the cooled roast.
Repeat the layers, finishing with a layer of custard on top.
Refrigerate the trifle for at least 2 hours before serving.
